Purpose: Observe the current ecosystem state from stakeholders, transactions, scenarios, and metrics
Primary Inputs: Demand, orders, vendor capacity, supplier stock, delivery capacity, RMOV accounts, PSKVE ledger, scenario state, historical metrics
Internal Processing / Algorithms: Data collection, validation, aggregation, normalization, anomaly detection, state-vector construction
Example Variables: D(t), C_i, R_i(t), O_i(t), V_i(t), M_i(t), waste(t), satisfaction(t)
Decision Questions Supported: What is happening now? Which stakeholders are overloaded, underused, or at risk?
Contribution to Coordination: Creates shared situational awareness across stakeholders
Campus Food Example: Collect lunch demand, vendor production, supplier delivery status, and student queue signals

Purpose: Interpret the perceived state, predict consequences, and identify risks or opportunities
Primary Inputs: Observed state S(t), historical trends, scenario parameters, stakeholder models, mission goals G(t)
Internal Processing / Algorithms: Demand forecasting, bottleneck detection, shortage-risk estimation, waste-risk estimation, mission-dependency analysis, scenario projection
Primary Outputs: Interpreted state, predicted demand, predicted bottlenecks, risk indicators, opportunity indicators
Decision Questions Supported: What will happen next if no intervention is made? Where are the critical mission dependencies?
Contribution to Coordination: Transforms raw data into actionable ecosystem knowledge
Campus Food Example: Predict that exam week will create a demand surge and supplier bottleneck

Purpose: Select coordinated recommendations or policies that improve ecosystem value and mission achievement
Primary Inputs: Interpreted state, predicted risks, candidate MSRS policies, stakeholder utilities, constraints, EVI weights
Internal Processing / Algorithms: Multi-objective optimization, rule-based policy selection, MSRS recommendation generation, constraint checking, trade-off analysis
Primary Outputs: Recommendation vector Rec(t) and selected policy π_k
Example Variables: Rec_student(t), Rec_vendor(t), Rec_supplier(t), Rec_operator(t), Rec_regulator(t), π_k
Decision Questions Supported: Which recommendations should be issued to which stakeholders? Which policy is best under this scenario?
Contribution to Coordination: Converts ecosystem understanding into coordinated stakeholder-level actions
Campus Food Example: Recommend vendors to adjust production, suppliers to advance delivery, and students to choose less congested vendors

Purpose: Deliver recommendations to stakeholders and execute accepted actions in the ecosystem
Primary Inputs: Recommendation vector Rec(t), stakeholder acceptance probabilities, operational constraints
Internal Processing / Algorithms: Recommendation dispatch, stakeholder acceptance model, action execution, mission intensity update, RMOV update, PSKVE exchange recording
Primary Outputs: Accepted actions A_i(t), updated mission intensities M_i(t), executed outputs O_i(t)
Decision Questions Supported: Which stakeholders accepted recommendations? What actions were actually executed?
Contribution to Coordination: Implements the coordinated plan while preserving stakeholder autonomy
Campus Food Example: Vendor_A accepts recommendation to produce at 90% capacity; Supplier_1 advances delivery

Purpose: Evaluate outcomes, update models, and improve future recommendations
Primary Inputs: Executed actions, realized demand, served meals, waste, profit, satisfaction, mission achievement, EVI, stakeholder feedback
Internal Processing / Algorithms: Outcome evaluation, error analysis, policy scoring, model update, weight adjustment, reinforcement or adaptive learning placeholder
Primary Outputs: Updated policy knowledge, updated parameters, performance log, robustness evidence
Example Variables: EVI(t), MA(t), waste(t), profit(t), prediction_error(t), policy_score_k(t)
Decision Questions Supported: Did the intervention improve the ecosystem? What should be changed next time?
Contribution to Coordination: Enables continuous improvement and ecosystem adaptation across scenarios
Campus Food Example: After observing reduced waste but lower satisfaction, the system adjusts EVI weights or recommendation policy

Purpose: Maintain coherence among perception, understanding, decision, action, and learning over time
Primary Inputs: All PUDAL stage outputs, ecosystem goals, stakeholder constraints, scenario library
Internal Processing / Algorithms: Closed-loop control, experiment management, scenario comparison, robustness ranking, audit logging
Primary Outputs: Coordinated ecosystem trajectory, experimental history, scenario-policy performance table
Example Variables: S(t), Rec(t), A(t), Metrics(t), RI_k, EVI_bar_k
Decision Questions Supported: Which ecosystem architecture and MSRS policy is robust across scenarios?
Contribution to Coordination: Turns local stakeholder operations into a controlled mission-oriented ecosystem
Campus Food Example: Compare Baseline, Online Platform, and MOEE under normal, inflation, and multi-shock scenarios
